behaviors.description: The traveling-salesman heuristic produces a route that is no more than 1.5x the length of the optimal route for inputs up to 20 stops.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is algorithm quality inside the heuristic/route-scoring code, with no required module handoff, I/O, OS, network, subprocess, or AI model output. Forcing phrase: "heuristic produces a route ... no more than 1.5x ... optimal" targets computation correctness. Adjacent-lower miss-proof: this is already the lowest level (Unit). Rejection evidence for higher levels: behavior text contains no external infrastructure requirement and no multi-module orchestration requirement.
behaviors.test_description: Call the heuristic function directly with fixed coordinate sets (including known benchmark instances with precomputed optimal lengths) and assert produced-route-length / optimal-length <= 1.5 for each case up to 20 stops.

behaviors.description: The delivery time window estimates are computed correctly from the distance between consecutive stops and the configured average speed for the road type.
behaviors.justification: Primary failure boundary is deterministic formula/mapping logic (time = distance/speed by road type), which is testable within one estimator boundary. Forcing phrase: "computed correctly from the distance ... and configured average speed" describes local computation. Rejection evidence for Integration: no explicit requirement that failure appears only at module handoff; passing distance + speed config into one object/function can expose real bugs.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke the time-window estimator with table-driven leg distances and road-type speed config (including edge cases like mixed road types and rounding boundaries) and assert exact expected leg and total time windows.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Would still fail at Unit if conversion, lookup, or rounding logic is wrong; no higher infrastructure is required by this behavior text.

behaviors.description: The shift-hours validator rejects a route whose total estimated time exceeds the driver's remaining shift hours, returning the excess amount.
behaviors.justification: This behavior is pure validation arithmetic and threshold logic at one object boundary. Forcing phrase: "rejects ... exceeds ... returning the excess amount" is local decision + calculation. Rejection evidence for higher levels: no OS/network/subprocess, no AI dependency, and no required multi-module interaction in the behavior statement.
behaviors.test_description: Call the validator with total-estimated-time and remaining-shift-hours inputs around boundary values (equal, just over, far over) and assert reject/accept result plus exact excess time value.

behaviors.description: When one address in the list fails geocoding (API returns no results), the service skips that stop and optimizes the remaining addresses, reporting which addresses were skipped.
behaviors.justification: Real failure appears at local module handoffs: geocoding result handling -> skip list -> optimizer input -> report output. Forcing phrase: "service skips that stop and optimizes the remaining addresses, reporting which addresses were skipped" requires cross-module state propagation. Integration-brake checks: (a) real OS/network/subprocess not required because a deterministic in-process geocoder fake can return "no results" exactly; (b) no AI/ML output dependency; (c) no multi-agentic chain. Rejection evidence for System: behavior specifies API semantic outcome ("returns no results"), not transport-level failures that require real HTTP.
behaviors.test_description: Wire service modules with a stub geocoder that returns coordinates for most addresses and empty results for one address; execute service call and assert (1) failed address is in skipped list, (2) optimizer receives only geocoded stops, and (3) returned route excludes skipped stop.

behaviors.description: Duplicate addresses in the input list are consolidated into a single stop with combined delivery items, not visited twice.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is interaction between deduplication/aggregation and route construction: consolidation must alter downstream visited stops. Forcing phrase: "consolidated into a single stop ... not visited twice" implies state handoff across modules, not just one helper function. Integration-brake checks: no real OS/network/subprocess requirement, no AI dependency, no multi-agentic sequence. Rejection evidence for System/Agentic/Workflow: behavior text is deterministic local processing.
behaviors.test_description: Run the service pipeline with repeated address inputs carrying different delivery items; assert output has one stop for that address, combined item payload, and route visit count for that location equals one.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: A pure Unit test of only dedupe logic could miss regression where optimizer still receives duplicated stops; Integration is the minimum to catch that real handoff failure.
